Title: Banana MaNAC1 activates secondary cell wall cellulose biosynthesis to enhance chilling resistance in fruit

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose accumulation in banana peel under chilling stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaCESA6B transcript abundance was significantly induced in banana peel after chilling treatment. 2. Its promoter contains SNBE motifs bound by MaNAC1 in EMSA. 3. MaNAC1 activated MaCESA6B promoter activity in transient assays, linking MaCESA6B to chilling-induced cellulose deposition.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell wall architecture and contributes to chilling resistance in fruit
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cellulose biosynthesis / secondary cell wall biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary and secondary cell wall cellulose synthase module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes glucose residues from UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan chains of cellulose microfibrils
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in banana peel under chilling stress, especially at later stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in banana peel under chilling stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript profiling under chilling; promoter cis-element analysis; EMSA for MaNAC1 binding; dual-luciferase transactivation assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MaCESA6B promoter in reporter assay; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for internal REN and transgene constructs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate cellulose-biosynthesis gene for engineering stronger fruit peel cell walls and improved chilling tolerance, especially in combination with MaNAC1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome data of banana peel under chilling at d0, d0.25, d0.5, d1, d3, d5; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains one or more low-temperature responsive elements and SNBE motifs; discussed as a MaNAC1 direct target.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose accumulation and secondary wall formation in banana peel under chilling stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaCESA7 was significantly induced in banana peel during late chilling stages. 2. MaNAC1 directly bound SNBE motifs in the MaCESA7 promoter by EMSA. 3. MaNAC1 activated MaCESA7 transcription in transient assays, and the paper highlights MaCESA7 as a predominant contributor to secondary wall cellulose synthesis under chilling injury.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides mechanical strength to the cell wall and enhances fruit chilling tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cellulose biosynthesis / secondary cell wall biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Secondary cell wall cellulose synthase complex module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes synthesis of β-1,4-glucan chains for cellulose microfibril formation in the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in banana peel at later chilling stages (d3-d5)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in banana peel under chilling stress
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control component of secondary wall cellulose synthesis, but formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript profiling under chilling; qRT-PCR; EMSA for direct MaNAC1-promoter binding; dual-luciferase transactivation assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MaCESA7 promoter in reporter assay; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for internal REN and transgene constructs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for improving fruit peel cell wall robustness and chilling resistance, especially through activation by MaNAC1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome data of banana peel under chilling at d0, d0.25, d0.5, d1, d3, d5; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as likely having a predominant role in secondary cell wall cellulose synthase complexes under chilling conditions.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Musa acuminata; Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose accumulation, thickens cell walls, and enhances chilling resistance in fruit
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaNAC1 is a cold-inducible NAC transcription factor in banana peel and a homolog of Arabidopsis secondary wall NACs/SND3. 2. Overexpression in Arabidopsis increased cell wall thickness and cellulosic glucan content and complemented the snd3 mutant secondary wall defect. 3. MaNAC1 directly binds SNBE motifs in MaCESA6B and MaCESA7 promoters and activates their transcription. 4. Overexpression in tomato fruit increased cellulose content, thickened peel cell walls, induced SlCESA genes, and improved chilling tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural reinforcement of cell walls and protection against chilling injury
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Banana peel cell wall; Arabidopsis stem cell wall; Tomato fruit peel cell wall
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor; secondary wall NAC (SWN) family; homolog of SND3
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cellulose biosynthesis and secondary cell wall formation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive modulator and pivotal regulator of chilling-induced cellulose deposition in banana fruit cell walls
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MaCESA6B and MaCESA7 transcription by binding SNBE motifs in their promoters; also associated with increased expression of other CESA genes in heterologous systems
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly upregulated in postharvest banana peel under chilling stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In banana peel induced by chilling; in transgenic Arabidopsis and tomato overexpressed from CaMV 35S promoter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low temperature / chilling injury at 7 °C in banana and 4 °C in Arabidopsis and tomato
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression analysis; phylogenetic comparison; transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis and tomato; complementation of Arabidopsis snd3 mutant; cell wall thickness measurement; monosaccharide/cellulose analysis; EMSA; dual-luciferase transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native MaCESA6B and MaCESA7 promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding or engineering chilling-tolerant fruit crops through reinforcement of peel cell walls and activation of cellulose biosynthesis; useful transgene/regulatory target for postharvest cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In Arabidopsis overexpression altered growth traits such as longer roots and enhanced secondary growth; broader wall remodeling may have developmental effects
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Banana peel transcriptome under chilling stress; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported as a direct target of MaICE1 and interacting with MaCBF1. The paper focuses on MaNAC1 as the central regulator linking cold signaling to cellulose biosynthesis.
